feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Depreciation Posting AFAB S4HANA BADIS FAA_DC_CUSTOMER, FAA_EE_CUSTOME not work
entry.content:
Best regards.

We come to you because we have a situation in the implementation of Badis FAA_DC_CUSTOMER and FAA_EE_CUSTOMER for the S4HANA environment.

The Badis mentioned above have been implemented in the ECC environment and have traditionally been running on transactions: ABNAN, ABAON, ABAVN, ABZU, ABMA, ABAA, ABAW, ABZON, AS02, AS01, ABUMN, AB08, AFAR and AFAB.

Currently, logic to customize the ECC processes has been implemented in the above mentioned badis and transactions:

  • DC_CLOSED_YEARS_CALC
  • DC_RETIREMENT_REQUEST
  • DC_TRANSACTION_POSTING
  • DC_RECALCULATION
  • DC_TRANSACTION_CALCULATE
  • DC_DEPRECIATION_POSTING

Specifically the finding we have is under the transaction AFAB due to the fact that in the S4HANA environment the mentioned badis (FAA_DC_CUSTOMER, FAA_EE_CUSTOMER) are not executed.

In the ECC environment, for transaction AFAB we identified that the Badis are executed for the process “DC_DEPRECATION_POSTING” and the sub-process “DC_DEPRECATION_POSTING”.

We identified the interface IF_FAA_DC_SEGMENTS_720_C which uses the FI-AA component to declare the value of the processes and steps that are used in S4HANA, however it was not possible to identify which of these would replace or be the equivalent of the ECC process and subprocess “DC_DEPRECATION_POSTING”Fixed assets

entry.title: CJEN deletes value type 11 (statistical actual) in RPSCO table
entry.content:
I made postings via tcode KAFD as part of our migration on August 2024.  Recently,  I have implemented SAP note 853530 - Residual order plan should not be taken into account to address issue on RemOrdPlan.  One of the instructions after the sap note is implemented is to run CJEN where check box "delete" was unticked and CJBV.  After i run CJEN, I noticed that all my postings made via KAFD with value type 11 were deleted and this resulted to incorrect Actual Cost in CN41N.

 

How can I restore those postings in RPSCO given that when I ran CJEN,  delete checkbox was not ticked.  How come it deletes value type 11?

entry.title: Error getting technical receiver for outbound service EDI_ORDERCONFIRMATIONREQUEST_OUT_SPRX
entry.content:
Hi Experts

We are trying to trigger the SOAP API CO_EDI_ORDER_CONF_REQUEST for Sales Order Confirmation as an EDI output, this is an outbound scenario to send Sales order confirmation from SAP S/4HANA (Private Cloud) to SAP Integration Suite which will further map the message and send to 3rd party system.

but encountering an issue where the service is not being called, we are getting this error in the output processing,

Error getting technical receiver for outbound service EDI_ORDERCONFIRMATIONREQUEST_OUT_SPRX

  • The Logical port has been created in SOAMANAGER
  • NACE configuration has been done to configure the respective program and form routine as per SAP documentation. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/cf829dd7c1ef4eb08eca4575c2e518ff.html

In Debugging via program RSNAST00:

The code is trying to determine provider applications (technical receiver) in the below code, which fails and this error is thrown, any idea how and where to maintain the provider application/technical receiver?

Any documentation link will be helpful.

 

Thanks

Deepak

entry.title: PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP - October 2024
entry.content:
Welcome to the PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP Blog!

We're excited to introduce you to our monthly newsletter, your go-to resource for staying connected with the latest innovations, insights, and trends in the RISE with SAP ecosystem. Each edition is thoughtfully curated to provide exclusive expert guidance, inspiring stories, and actionable content designed to help you enhance your skills and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP.

Whether you're looking to explore emerging technologies or gain valuable knowledge tailored to your needs, our newsletter has you covered. We also encourage you to share this resource with your network and become an active member of our vibrant partner community.

Thank you for your continued support! Should you have any feedback or questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to help you make the most of your RISE with SAP journey.

Stay tuned, stay informed, and sign up today! 

Let's grow together!

 

Victor Echeverri Reyes                                                               

 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Partner Delivery Manager                                                                                   Ecosystem Delivery Success   
 E: victor.echeverri.reyes@sap.com

entry.title: Local consumption of business event in S/4HANA ABAP cloud
entry.content:
Could you please explain the below with Example. In My case I am using R_SalesOrderTP . But while implementing in through eclipse it is saying The use of Behavior Definition R_SALESORDERTP is not permitted. I came accrose one more question that as it is i am pasting here. 


"I came across the SAP documentation regarding local consumption of standard business events

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/business-event-consumption?version=s4hana_cloud

And it was highlighted that the event to be consumed needs to be exposed in a C1 released RAP BO interface.

Now I am wondering, are all these events from the accelerator hub for s/4hana cloud based on RAP BO model? And if not, where/how could some of these events were defined/raised?

https://api.sap.com/package/SAPS4HANACloudBusinessEvents/event

Also, is there a way for us to find the behavior definition where the event was defined? Or for that we will need to have access to a live S/4HANA cloud system?"

entry.title: Overlapping fields in the pop-up associated with the occasional customer when creating a sales order
entry.content:
Hi

I'm having a problem with the occasional customer, i.e. when I put the sold-to party “sporadic” in the sales order, when I open the pop-up to change the data associated with the customer at the time of the sale, overlapping fields appear, in particular the field relating to the tax number.

 

As you can see in the image, the other attributes tab and its fields appear overlapping, making it impossible to fill in those fields.

I don't know if this error has to do with the system version, as it's S/4HANA, version 102.

Has anyone had this problem?

Thanks

entry.title: Not able to convert account to open item managed
entry.content: When we try to convert account 231013 for company code 1007 in SH3. i am getting error.

entry.title: Difference between S/4HANA private cloud vs S/4HANA on-premise
entry.content:
As most people say, S/4HANA private cloud is almost similar to on-premise edition except that the private cloud solution is managed by SAP. What are the other major differences that we need to take into account for? (e.g., topics like security, how to expose custom oData service and how to authenticate, etc.)

With public cloud, when exposing a web API, we usually create the communication arrangements/scenario/system/users. Do we have the same functionality in S/4HANA private cloud edition?

Even though the S/4HANA private cloud is managed by SAP, is it still considered as "on-premise"? Where should the cloud connector software be installed?

entry.title: Excel template in BTP BAS tool giving error when deploying app
entry.content:
We are creating an app to upload data from an excel and update the backend custom table using BTP BAS and oData.

To keep the loaded data correct, we are giving option to download a template in excel format and upload the same with relevant data. When trying to deploy the app to ABAP environment with the excel template in BAS, its giving error and not recognizing the template. 

If we remove the template specific code and deploy it to ABAP repository, its getting deployed successfully.

Is there any missing configuration to allow template based excel upload via BAS.

entry.title: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting - Part 1
entry.content:
To help you automate SAP post-refresh steps, I'll provide an outline based on commonly used SAP T-codes like SU01, SM21, SE06, SCC4, RZ10, RZ04, and AL11. These are essential T-codes that come into play after refreshing an SAP system. In this article, I will describe how to automate each step using code examples (e.g., scripts or SAP Automation Frameworks such as SAP GUI scripting or ABAP) and explain how it helps reduce manual effort and human errors.

1. Understanding Post-Refresh Steps and Manual Effort

Post-refresh steps in SAP are crucial to ensure that a newly refreshed system (usually from production to quality or development) functions correctly with the required configurations. These steps often include resetting or configuring system settings, adjusting roles and authorizations, and cleaning up logs and profiles.

Manual execution of these steps across multiple systems leads to:

  • Human errors: Mistakes can easily occur, such as setting incorrect parameters or missing a step.
  • Repetitive tasks: Doing the same post-refresh tasks in each system is tedious and time-consuming.
  • Lack of consistency: Different individuals performing these tasks may interpret instructions differently.

By automating post-refresh steps, we reduce manual interaction, ensuring consistent and accurate system configurations, saving time, and avoiding errors.

2. Automation for Each T-Code

Let's now explore automation for each T-code from the provided file, including script examples and how they reduce manual work.

T-Code: SU01 - User Management (Role Adjustment)

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "SU01"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtS_USER").Text = "USERNAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]").Press 'Display user
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subPERSONAS_AREA:SAPLSUU5:0300/txtSU01_ROLES").Text = "NEW_ROLE" 'Assign new role
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Save changes

How It Helps:

  • Automates role assignment and user management.
  • Ensures all team members have correct roles in the refreshed system.
  • Reduces errors like assigning wrong roles to users.

T-Code: SM21 - System Log (Log Cleanup)

Automation Script Example (ABAP Report to Delete Logs):

abap
CALL FUNCTION 'RSSM_LOG_DELETE'
  EXPORTING
    log_number = 'ALL' " Deletes all logs
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS = 1.

How It Helps:

  • Automatically cleans up logs after a refresh.
  • Reduces time spent manually reviewing and deleting unnecessary logs.
  • Ensures that the system is fresh with no outdated logs.

T-Code: SE06 - System Change Option (System Change Configuration)

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "SE06"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/radRAD_MOD_ON").Select
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[0]").Press 'Enable modifications for the system

How It Helps:

  • Automates system change settings.
  • Ensures that system configurations are set correctly after the refresh.
  • Reduces the risk of forgetting to unlock or lock the system for changes.

T-Code: SCC4 - Client Administration (Client Configuration)

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "SCC4"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/lbl[0]").Press 'Select client
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtPFL_0010-CLIENT_ROLE").Text = "PRODUCTION" 'Set client role
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Save changes

How It Helps:

  • Automates the process of setting client roles.
  • Ensures that client-specific settings are accurate after refresh, avoiding improper client roles.
  • Saves time by setting roles programmatically instead of manually.

T-Code: RZ10 - Profile Parameter Management

Automation Script Example (ABAP Script to Change Profile Parameters):

abap
CALL FUNCTION 'RZ10_PARM_MAINTAIN'
  EXPORTING
    profile_name = 'DEFAULT.PFL'
    parameter_name = 'rdisp/mshost'
    parameter_value = 'new_value'
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS = 1.

How It Helps:

  • Ensures that all necessary profile parameters are correctly updated post-refresh.
  • Reduces manual errors in setting parameters by automating the process.
  • Can be scheduled to update multiple parameters at once, saving time.

T-Code: RZ04 - Operation Modes and Instances

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "RZ04"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtS_OPER_MODE").Text = "DEFAULT_MODE"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Save operation mode

How It Helps:

  • Automates the configuration of operation modes.
  • Ensures that the system switches to the correct operation modes without manual intervention.
  • Reduces the likelihood of incorrect settings that could affect system performance.

T-Code: AL11 - Directory Display

Automation Script Example (SAP GUI Scripting for Directory Check):

vbs
Set session = SAPGuiApp.Children(0).Children(0)
session.StartTransaction "AL11"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtDIR_NAME").Text = "/usr/sap/trans" 'Change to required directory
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").Press 'Display contents of the directory

How It Helps:

  • Automatically checks directory contents to ensure that post-refresh transport or system files are available.
  • Reduces the time spent manually navigating and checking directories.
  • Can be scheduled to run periodically to check directory consistency.

3. Benefits of Automation

By automating these post-refresh steps, several key benefits can be realized:

  • Error Reduction: Automation ensures that each step is executed correctly every time, reducing the likelihood of human error.
  • Time Savings: Scripts run much faster than manual intervention, particularly when dealing with large systems.
  • Consistency: Automated steps ensure consistent execution across systems, leading to predictable outcomes.
  • Audit Trail: Automation scripts can be logged, providing an auditable record of actions taken.

4. Conclusion

Automating SAP post-refresh steps such as user management (SU01), log cleanup (SM21), system change configurations (SE06), and profile updates (RZ10) can significantly reduce the manual effort and errors associated with post-refresh tasks. By using SAP GUI scripting or ABAP automation, these processes can be streamlined, ensuring consistent, accurate, and fast post-refresh operations. Organizations implementing automation for these steps will benefit from increased system stability and reduced downtime.

entry.title: How to create query reports
Is it possible to perform HU creation during Transfer Posting in EWM?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on a scenario where my Storage type is HU managed.

Process : 

MIGO from S4 with 311 from SLOC A to SLOC B ,both EWM managed

Once received the the Transfer posting document in EWM>> create WT>>Confirm WT.

But as my PUTAWAY storage Type is HU managed with HU creation is not posible to create HU. system is giving error 

"no bin search possible. storage types/sections/bin types incomplete" 

Here my question is can i perfrom HU creation during the WT creation using PACKSPEC . i tried it but system is not creating any HU.

Thanks

Arjun

entry.title: Find an API I can use to get data from 'Manage Supplier Line Items' in S/4HANA
entry.content: I am seeking an API that can retrieve data from the ‘Manage Supplier Line Items’ module in S/4HANA. I attempted to use the sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/A_SupplierInvoice API; however, it does not provide the information I require. I have been informed that the invoices retrieved using this API are from the Supplier Invoices List, which includes invoices raised from a Purchase Order. It does not encompass other types of invoices, such as direct invoices.

entry.title: SAP MDG: How to make grey / non-editable any fields on UI ?
entry.content:
Hi SCN People, 

 

How to make any of the fields in UI (grey / non-editable) based on Field. 

Any Inputs or resources will be helpful.

 

Thank You, 
Suggu Sandeep.

entry.title: No printout for FINT cancellation invoice
entry.content:
Hi

When cancelling an interest invoice using FINTSHOW, a credit printout of the cancellation invoice should be generated, as it is a legal requirement to send the customer a credit invoice.

However, FINTSHOW only generates the cancellation document and doesn't print it. It is not possible to use the reprint function for the cancellation invoice, since it is not displayed in FINTSHOW.

Thanks

entry.title: What application is S/4HANA Cloud do I use to view/modify/update "purchasing group" characteristics?
entry.content: I would like to view/modify/update some information for our only purchasing group in S/4HANA. Specifically, we have one "Group 001" that has never been updated but has information like Phone number, Fax, Email, etc. that displays on all of our purchase orders. This information is not valid and, therefore, I would like to update to reflect current state. I've tried searching but can't find out how to make changes. Thank you so much!

entry.title: Find all WBS element related to a WBS node in a CDS
entry.content:
Hello,

In a CDS, I read another CDS giving me a list of WBS nodes. How could I find all the WBS related to a node. Ex :

I received the node C.00103.1 and get in return C.00103.1.1 and C.00103.1.2.3

C.00103.1

   C.00103.1.1

   C.00103.1.2.3

C.00103.2

   C.00103.2.1 etc

II had a look in table PRHI but it doesn't give what I want.

Thanks in advance

Mat

entry.title: Posting stock from f2 to z2
entry.content:
Hello All,

I want to know whether any of you have faced the situation where you have got multiple Storage Locations in S4 Hana which are mapped to one EWM warehouse number resulting in creation of multiple Availability Group as mapping of RP storage location to EWM can't have same Availability Group for two Different Storage Locations.

In such scenario where we Perform SLOC to SLoc transfer via 1 step which result in pointing stock from Stock type F2 to Z* .in such case if storage type have the availability group assigned then it result in error

But if WPT is determining the Availability group that is it possible to perform the posting form F2 to Z* or we need badi implementation?

entry.title: Query about depreciation calculation in fixed assets
entry.content:
Hola a todos,

En 2020, implementamos SAP y durante la carga inicial de activos fijos, los activos antiguos se ingresaron con fechas incorrectas. Los activos que eran del año 2015 se registraron con fecha de adquisición en 2020. (Imagen 1)

Corregimos la fecha de inicio de la depreciación a la correcta (30 de abril de 2015) y recalculamos los valores. (Imagen 2)

Sin embargo, al revisar los nuevos valores de depreciación, no parecen tener sentido. (Imagen 3)

El tema es que en agosto hay un monto diferente y el resto se prorratea entre los demás meses. Mis preguntas hijo:

  1. ¿Por qué SAP calculó un monto diferente para agosto y luego lo prorrateó entre los meses siguientes?
  2. ¿Cuál es la base de estos cálculos en SAP?

Esperábamos que toda la diferencia de depreciación se registrara en agosto y que a partir de los meses siguientes se reflejara el monto correcto de $ -777,238.

El activo se cargó con los siguientes detalles:

Imagen 4

Imagen 5

El importe que debió depreciarse es el siguiente:

  • Valor de compra: $93,268,568
  • Meses: 120
  • Depreciación mensual: $-777,238

Agradecería mucho cualquier ayuda para comprender y resolver este problema.

Atentamente,

Aurora.

entry.title: Can you delete a G/L account in Quality System S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello,

We created a G/l Account in our quality (test) system but realize we don't need it anymore. How can we delete it completely before transporting the other system changes? S/4HANA Cloud.
